Operation 'cancelShipment' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects, missing_output_details. The current description is too minimal and lacks critical context for AI agents. Added information about when cancellation is applicable, what parameters are required, what the operation returns, and important constraints about shipment state eligibility. This helps AI agents understand the operation's scope and limitations.

data.current_description: Cancel a shipment by its ID.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels an active shipment by its ID. This operation is used to cancel orders that have not yet been delivered. Requires the shipment ID as a path parameter. Returns confirmation of the cancellation with updated shipment status. Note: Cancellation may only be possible for shipments in specific states (e.g., pending, processing). Returns an error if the shipment cannot be cancelled.
data.reasoning: The current description is too minimal and lacks critical context for AI agents. Added information about when cancellation is applicable, what parameters are required, what the operation returns, and important constraints about shipment state eligibility. This helps AI agents understand the operation's scope and limitations.

message: Operation 'approveReturn' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects, missing_output_details. Original description lacks operational context and side effects. Enhanced version explains the approval workflow, what parameters are needed, what is returned, and importantly, the side effects (refund initiation, label generation, inventory updates) that help AI agents understand the full impact of this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Approves a customer return request by its ID. This operation authorizes the return and typically initiates the refund process. Requires the return ID as a path parameter. Returns confirmation of the approval with updated return status. Side effects include: initiating refund processing, generating return shipping labels, and updating inventory. The operation may fail if the return is already processed or expired.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks operational context and side effects. Enhanced version explains the approval workflow, what parameters are needed, what is returned, and importantly, the side effects (refund initiation, label generation, inventory updates) that help AI agents understand the full impact of this operation.

message: Operation 'rejectReturn' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects, missing_output_details. Similar to approveReturn, the original description is incomplete. Enhanced version clarifies the rejection workflow, required parameters, return value, and critical side effects (refund cancellation, customer notification, inventory retention) that help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation appropriately.

data.description_suggestion: Rejects a customer return request by its ID. This operation denies the return and prevents refund processing. Requires the return ID as a path parameter. Returns confirmation of the rejection with updated return status. Side effects include: cancelling any associated refund, notifying the customer of rejection, and retaining inventory. The operation may fail if the return is already processed or if rejection is not permitted for the return type.
data.reasoning: Similar to approveReturn, the original description is incomplete. Enhanced version clarifies the rejection workflow, required parameters, return value, and critical side effects (refund cancellation, customer notification, inventory retention) that help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation appropriately.

message: Operation 'createListings' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too generic. Enhanced version specifies what the request body should contain (using the CreateListingsRequest schema context), what is returned, important side effects (marketplace publication, searchability, inventory reservation), and validation constraints (category requirements, duplicate prevention) that help AI agents understand the operation's full scope.

data.current_description: Create new product listings on the Flipkart marketplace.
data.description_suggestion: Creates one or more new product listings on the Flipkart marketplace. Accepts a CreateListingsRequest containing product details (SKU, title, description, pricing, inventory). Returns a response with listing IDs and status for each created listing. Side effects include: publishing products to the marketplace, making them searchable and purchasable, and reserving inventory. Listings must comply with category-specific requirements. Duplicate SKUs will be rejected.
data.reasoning: Current description is too generic. Enhanced version specifies what the request body should contain (using the CreateListingsRequest schema context), what is returned, important side effects (marketplace publication, searchability, inventory reservation), and validation constraints (category requirements, duplicate prevention) that help AI agents understand the operation's full scope.

message: Operation 'updateListings' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specificity about inputs and outputs. Enhanced version clarifies the request structure (UpdateListingsRequest), what fields can be updated, the partial update capability, return value, and side effects (marketplace visibility changes, search ranking impact, inventory adjustments) that help AI agents understand the operation's behavior and implications.

data.description_suggestion: Updates one or more existing product listings on the Flipkart marketplace. Accepts an UpdateListingsRequest containing listing IDs and fields to modify (pricing, inventory, description, etc.). Returns a response with update status for each listing. Supports partial updates - only provided fields are modified. Side effects include: updating marketplace visibility, affecting search rankings, and adjusting available inventory. Changes take effect immediately. Invalid listing IDs will be rejected.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about inputs and outputs. Enhanced version clarifies the request structure (UpdateListingsRequest), what fields can be updated, the partial update capability, return value, and side effects (marketplace visibility changes, search ranking impact, inventory adjustments) that help AI agents understand the operation's behavior and implications.

message: Operation 'getAccessToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks critical details about authentication requirements, grant type use cases, and response structure. Enhanced version clarifies which grant type to use when, authentication method (BasicAuth), and how to use the returned token, improving AI agent decision-making for token acquisition scenarios.

data.current_description: Generate an access token using client credentials, authorization code, or refresh token grant types.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate OAuth access token for API authentication
data.description_suggestion: Generates an OAuth 2.0 access token for authenticating subsequent API requests. Supports three grant types: client_credentials (for app-to-app authentication), authorization_code (for user delegation), and refresh_token (to extend session). Requires BasicAuth with appId:appSecret. Returns TokenResponse containing the access token, token type, expiration time, and refresh token. The returned token should be used in the Authorization header for subsequent requests.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks critical details about authentication requirements, grant type use cases, and response structure. Enhanced version clarifies which grant type to use when, authentication method (BasicAuth), and how to use the returned token, improving AI agent decision-making for token acquisition scenarios.

message: Operation 'checkTokenExpiry' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original description is incomplete regarding input requirements and response structure. Enhanced version specifies that the token is passed as input, clarifies the response format (TokenExpiryResponse), explains the use case (proactive refresh), and notes that this is a read-only operation with no side effects.

data.current_description: Check whether an access or refresh token has expired.
data.summary_suggestion: Check if OAuth token has expired
data.description_suggestion: Checks whether an access token or refresh token has expired without consuming the token. Accepts the token to validate as a parameter. Returns TokenExpiryResponse indicating expiration status and remaining time-to-live (TTL) if applicable. Useful for proactive token refresh before making API calls. Does not modify token state.
data.reasoning: Original description is incomplete regarding input requirements and response structure. Enhanced version specifies that the token is passed as input, clarifies the response format (TokenExpiryResponse), explains the use case (proactive refresh), and notes that this is a read-only operation with no side effects.

data.current_description: Search and filter shipments by various criteria.
data.description_suggestion: Searches and filters shipments across your seller account using ShipmentFilterRequest criteria. Supports filtering by shipment status, date range, order ID, and other attributes. Returns ShipmentListResponse containing matching shipments with pagination support. Useful for monitoring order fulfillment status, identifying pending shipments, or generating shipment reports.

message: Operation 'getShipmentLabels' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description lacks context about label format, use case, and whether batch operations are supported. data.current_description: Retrieve shipping labels for a shipment.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves shipping labels for a specific shipment identified by shipmentId. Returns label data in formats suitable for printing (typically PDF or image format). Labels contain tracking information, barcode, and destination address. Required for physical shipment handoff to carrier. Supports batch label retrieval if multiple shipments are provided.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks context about label format, use case, and whether batch operations are supported. Enhanced version clarifies the output format, explains the business purpose (carrier handoff), and indicates batch capability, helping AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'getShipmentInvoices' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description lacks detail about invoice format, content, and business context. Enhanced version clarifies output format (PDF), explains invoice contents and purpose (fulfillment documentation, customer communication), and notes that multiple invoices may exist per shipment, improving AI agent understanding of when to retrieve invoices.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves invoice documents associated with a specific shipment identified by shipmentId. Returns invoice data in formats suitable for archival and customer communication (typically PDF). Invoices contain itemized product details, pricing, taxes, and payment information. Used for order fulfillment documentation and customer delivery. One or more invoices may be returned per shipment.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks detail about invoice format, content, and business context. Enhanced version clarifies output format (PDF), explains invoice contents and purpose (fulfillment documentation, customer communication), and notes that multiple invoices may exist per shipment, improving AI agent understanding of when to retrieve invoices.

message: Operation 'confirmDispatch' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too vague about what 'dispatch' means and lacks critical details. Enhanced version clarifies the business process (handoff to carrier), specifies input format (shipment IDs in request body), explains the state change (status update to 'dispatched'), mentions side effects (tracking sync), and warns about irreversibility, enabling AI agents to understand the operation's significance and consequences.

data.description_suggestion: Confirms that one or more shipments have been handed off to the carrier for delivery. Accepts shipment IDs in the request body. Updates shipment status to 'dispatched' in the system and triggers carrier tracking synchronization. Returns confirmation of processed shipments and any errors. This is a state-changing operation that notifies Flipkart that shipments are in transit. Once confirmed, shipment status cannot be reverted.
data.reasoning: Original description is too vague about what 'dispatch' means and lacks critical details. Enhanced version clarifies the business process (handoff to carrier), specifies input format (shipment IDs in request body), explains the state change (status update to 'dispatched'), mentions side effects (tracking sync), and warns about irreversibility, enabling AI agents to understand the operation's significance and consequences.
